Checkpoint 4.2.4. Matrix Representation of the Derivative Operator.

Let \(\sP_{k}\) denote the span of \(\{1, \cos t, \sin t, \ldots, \cos (kt), \sin (kt)\}\) and define \(D:\sP_{k}\mapsto \sP_{k}\) be the derivative operator.
  1. Find the matrix representation of \(D\) and \(D\circ D\) in the given basis.
  2. Does \(D\) map the span of \(\{ \cos t, \sin t, \ldots, \cos (kt), \sin (kt)\}\) to itself? If so, determine whether this map is invertible.
in-context
